title: Xtra Flaky Syrian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Syrian dish with a twist, featuring extra virgin olive oil and a flaky crust, this tagine is a herbal and adventurous delight, low in fat for a friendly meal option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the garlic, thyme, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the lemon juice and stir to combine.
Roll out the phyllo dough and cut it into 4 equal pieces.
Place a piece of the phyllo dough over the lamb mixture and brush with a little water.
Repeat the layering process with the remaining phyllo dough pieces, brushing each piece with a little water.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 325 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
